# Contributing to Rust-Red

Thank you for considering contributing to Rust-Red!

## How to Contribute

1. Fork the repository.

2. Clone the forked repository to your local machine:

    ```bash
    git clone --recursive https://github.com/dzulfikar08/rust-red.git
    cd rust-red
    ```

3. Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix:

    ```bash
    git checkout -b feature-name
    ```

4. Make your changes, and ensure they follow the coding style and guidelines.

5. Test your changes thoroughly:

    ```bash
    cargo test --all
    ```

6. Commit your changes:

    ```bash
    git commit -m "Your descriptive commit message"
    ```

7. Push your changes to your forked repository:

    ```bash
    git push origin feature-name
    ```

8. Create a pull request (PR) on the main repository.

## Code Style and Guidelines

- Follow the existing code style used in the project.
- Keep code changes focused and small for easier review.
- Provide clear and detailed commit messages.

## Reporting Issues

If you find a bug or have a feature request, please open an issue on the [issue tracker](https://github.com/dzulfikar08/rust-red/issues). Ensure that you provide as much detail as possible, including steps to reproduce the issue.

## Thank You!

Thank you for contributing to Rust-Red! Your time and effort are greatly appreciated.
